title: Genitive — Quantities & Negation
description: Practice the genitive case with quantity expressions (hodně, málo, trochu, kilo, litr + genitive plural) and learn the negation pattern where accusative objects shift to genitive (nemám peněz). Includes practical shopping and counting vocabulary.

exercises.prompt: What case does 'hodně' (a lot of) require?

data.question_en: What case does the word 'hodně' require?
data.explanation: 'Hodně' (a lot of) always requires the genitive case. Examples: hodně lidí (a lot of people — genitive plural), hodně práce (a lot of work — genitive singular). Same rule applies to málo (little/few) and trochu (a little).

exercises.prompt: Complete with the correct genitive form: 'I have a lot of work.'

data.explanation: 'Mám hodně práce.' = I have a lot of work. Práce (work, feminine) → práce (genitive singular). Note that 'práce' looks the same in nominative and genitive — this is typical for feminine nouns ending in -e/ě.

exercises.prompt: Translate to Czech: 'a lot of people'

data.grammar_note: 'Hodně lidí' = a lot of people. Lidé (people, nominative plural) → lidí (genitive plural). 'Hodně' + genitive plural for countable nouns: hodně knih, hodně aut, hodně studentů.

exercises.prompt: Complete the negative sentence correctly: 'I don't have money.'

data.explanation: 'Nemám peněz.' = I don't have money. When a sentence with the verb 'mít' (to have) is negated, the direct object shifts from accusative (peníze) to genitive (peněz). Peníze is a plurale tantum — it only exists in plural form. Genitive plural: peněz.

exercises.prompt: Complete with the genitive form after negation: 'I don't have time.'

data.explanation: 'Nemám čas.' or 'Nemám času.' = I don't have time. In negation, the accusative object of 'mít' can shift to genitive (času), though the accusative form (čas) is also common in everyday speech. For A2, both are accepted; the genitive času is the formal pattern.

exercises.prompt: Translate to Czech: 'I don't have money.'

data.grammar_note: 'Nemám peněz.' = I don't have money. Negation of 'mít' shifts the object from accusative (peníze) to genitive (peněz). You can also say 'Nemám žádné peníze.' using 'žádné' (no/none) for emphasis.

exercises.prompt: Complete the quantity phrase: 'a kilo of ___' (potatoes)

data.explanation: 'Kilo brambor' = a kilo of potatoes. After units of measurement (kilo, litr, gram, metr), use the genitive plural for countable items: brambory → brambor (genitive plural). Bramborů would be the genitive of the masculine form brambor, but brambora is feminine.

exercises.prompt: Complete with the correct genitive plural: 'I bought a kilo of apples.'

data.explanation: 'Koupil jsem kilo jablek.' = I bought a kilo of apples. Jablko (neuter) → jablka (nominative plural) → jablek (genitive plural). Note the vowel change: 'a' becomes 'e' in genitive plural — this is a common pattern for neuter nouns.

exercises.prompt: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence: 'I need a liter of milk.'

data.explanation: 'Potřebuju litr mléka.' = I need a liter of milk. Mléko (neuter) → mléka (genitive singular — mass nouns stay singular after units). 'Potřebovat' = to need.

exercises.prompt: Listen and write what you hear (negation and quantities).

data.expected_text: Nemám peněz. Mám jenom málo času.
data.note: 'Nemám peněz.' = I don't have money. 'Mám jenom málo času.' = I only have little time. 'Jenom' = only/just. 'Málo' + genitive: málo času (genitive singular), málo peněz (genitive plural).

exercises.answer_key: Nemám peněz. Mám jenom málo času.

data.target_text: Potřebuju kilo brambor, litr mléka a trochu cukru.
data.translation_en: I need a kilo of potatoes, a liter of milk, and a little sugar.

exercises.answer_key: Potřebuju kilo brambor, litr mléka a trochu cukru.

exercises.prompt: Complete the dialogue at a shop — using quantities and negation.

lines.speaker: zákazník
lines.text: ___ (I'd like a kilo of apples and a little bread.)

lines.speaker: zákazník
lines.text: ___ (No, thank you. I don't have more money.)

accepted_answers: Chtěl bych kilo jablek a trochu chleba.|Ne, děkuju. Nemám víc peněz.
Chtěla bych kilo jablek a trochu chleba.|Ne, děkuju. Nemám víc peněz.
data.explanation: 'Co si dáte?' = What would you like? (formal). 'Chtěl bych' = I would like (male); 'Chtěla bych' (female). 'Kilo jablek' = a kilo of apples (gen pl). 'Trochu chleba' = a little bread (gen sg). 'Nemám víc peněz.' = I don't have more money (negation → genitive).

exercises.answer_key: Chtěl bych kilo jablek a trochu chleba.|Ne, děkuju. Nemám víc peněz.
